title: Where Will Costco Stock Be in 3 Years?
author: Anders Bylund
description: New CEO Ron Vachris is maintaining Costco's traditional low-price philosophy while driving growth through AI integration and international expansion. The company is cutting prices despite inflation, expanding warehouse capacity, and achieving triple-digit growth in AI-driven search traffic. Same-day delivery now averages under 45 minutes with high conversion rates. At a 48 P/E ratio, Costco's premium valuation reflects investor confidence in its ability to blend cost discipline with next-generation technology.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CEO maintaining proven low-cost strategy while innovating with AI and expanding internationally. Strong operational metrics including triple-digit AI search growth, sub-45-minute same-day delivery, and plans for 30+ new warehouses annually. Premium valuation justified by growth drivers and member satisfaction.
Mentioned only as a valuation comparison point, trading at lower multiples than Costco. No specific operational or strategic information provided about the company.
Referenced only as a peer comparison for valuation purposes. No substantive analysis or commentary provided about the company's performance or strategy.

title: Costco Earnings Preview: Rising Fuel Prices Put Its Value Proposition to the Test
author: Ali Merchant
description: Costco is set to report Q3 earnings Thursday after the market close. While rising gasoline prices typically squeeze consumer budgets, they could actually benefit Costco by driving more member visits and in-store spending. The company has rallied 17% since the start of 2026 and faces lofty expectations. UBS raised its price target to $1,275 with a buy rating, citing robust results expected from consumers seeking cheaper gas and bargains. However, options market pricing suggests potential volatility of ±3% around earnings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong traffic growth from November to April, resilient consumer spending particularly among higher-income shoppers, higher gas prices expected to drive more visits and memberships, recent dividend boost, and multiple analyst upgrades with price targets of $1,160-$1,275 all support positive outlook for Q3 earnings.
Mentioned as performing well alongside Target, but no specific details or analysis provided about the company's performance or outlook.
Mentioned as performing well alongside Walmart, but no specific details or analysis provided about the company's performance or outlook.

Stock fell after reporting solid Q3 results, suggesting that even strong earnings may not prevent stock declines due to lofty market expectations, which could be a cautionary tale for Costco investors.

title: Why BJ’s Wholesale Club Stock Could Be Ready for a Rebound
author: Thomas Hughes
description: BJ's Wholesale Club presents a compelling investment opportunity with strong technical support, institutional accumulation, and solid fundamentals despite near-term headwinds. The stock has found a bottom and shows signs of recovery, supported by expanding store footprint, growing membership base, strong cash flow, and aggressive share buybacks. While Q1 guidance disappointed, the company's digital growth (28% YoY), membership expansion (9.9%), and fortress balance sheet position it well for future gains, with analyst consensus targeting approximately 25% upside.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong technical chart support with institutional accumulation, solid Q1 revenue growth (9.9%), robust digital growth (28% YoY), expanding membership base (9.9%), aggressive share buybacks reducing share count by 2.5% YoY, fortress balance sheet with low debt leverage, and strategic expansion into Texas. Analyst consensus provides ~25% upside potential despite near-term margin pressures.
Mentioned as a competitor in BJ's value proposition strategy but no specific analysis or sentiment indicators provided in the article.

title: NIO, Intuit, And Reddit Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (May 18-May 22): Are The Others In Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Large-cap stocks in software, electric vehicles, data centers, and healthcare faced significant pressure last week due to weak guidance, regulatory concerns, rising bond yields, and competitive risks. Technology and China-linked companies led the declines, with ten major stocks experiencing notable losses ranging from 4.96% to 18.28%.

title: S&P 500 Rally Defies Weak Sentiment and Hawkish Fed Signals
author: Itai Smidt
description: The S&P 500 rallied to new highs despite record-low consumer sentiment, hawkish Fed signals, and geopolitical tensions. The market is experiencing a broad rotation from mega-cap AI stocks to semiconductor suppliers, quantum computing names, and space-launch companies. Quantum computing stocks surged following a $2 billion Commerce Department investment, while chip suppliers and AI infrastructure plays outperformed. Yields retreated from recent highs, providing relief to equity multiples and enabling the rally to extend.
